Issue: Preloader obscured homepage hero on first mobile capture
Evidence: Initial live screenshot showed only nav/preloader/next section label after initial wait; fixed evidence in docs/qa/screenshots/home-390x844.png
Expected Behavior: Critical homepage content should be visible by default and animations must not gate content
Actual Behavior: Full-screen preloader could still cover hero if hydration started late
Likely Cause: Preloader used a full-screen bg-ink overlay and waited for client-side timing
Fix Applied: Changed preloader to a non-blocking logo treatment, removed the full-screen opaque background, and shortened the fade to 620ms
Retest Status: Pass on fixed local retest across all requested routes/viewports

Issue: Live Grace case study missing real animation/PDF assets
Evidence: Initial live pass reported missing Grace Animation Final text and /brand/grace/grace-branding-concept.pdf returned 404; fixed evidence in docs/qa/screenshots/work-grace-390x844.png
Expected Behavior: Grace case study should show the embedded animation and view/download PDF link
Actual Behavior: Production was behind the latest Grace asset commit
Likely Cause: Live deployment did not include the Grace MP4/PDF build
Fix Applied: Confirmed local build includes Grace video and PDF; fixed build retest verifies Grace text, video, and PDF asset across all requested viewports; deployed to production
Retest Status: Pass on fixed local retest and production spot check

Issue: Production contact copy still included old Book a call path
Evidence: Initial live contact screenshot showed "book a call"; fixed evidence in docs/qa/screenshots/contact-390x844.png
Expected Behavior: Only Start a project / direct email / work comparison should remain per client direction
Actual Behavior: Production was behind current repo copy
Likely Cause: Live deployment did not include the latest contact copy
Fix Applied: Current repo copy removes Book a call and routes users to email or work comparison; deployed to production
Retest Status: Pass on fixed local retest and production spot check

Issue: Contact form dead-ended when Web3Forms key is absent
Evidence: .env.example leaves NEXT_PUBLIC_WEB3FORMS_ACCESS_KEY empty and form previously only displayed an error
Expected Behavior: Contact form should still give users a working path to send a brief without silent failure
Actual Behavior: No production form key was discoverable in the local environment
Fix Applied: Added a mailto fallback that opens a prefilled project brief to hello@executionlabs.com and shows fallback-specific confirmation copy
Retest Status: Pass on targeted fixed local form retest

Issue: Mobile menu allowed underlying hero CTA to show through
Evidence: First mobile-menu screenshot showed menu CTA plus underlying hero Start a project CTA; fixed evidence in docs/qa/screenshots/mobile-menu-390x844.png
Expected Behavior: Mobile menu should be a clean focused panel with one primary CTA and no page content bleeding through
Actual Behavior: Menu panel only covered its content height and did not lock body scroll
Likely Cause: Mobile nav dropdown was in header flow instead of a viewport panel
Fix Applied: Changed mobile menu to a fixed panel from top nav to viewport bottom and locked body scroll while open
Retest Status: Pass on targeted fixed local menu retest
